---
title: "RichnessLatitude"
author: "Nicholas K. Dulvy"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
output: html_document
---

## Richness vs. latitude plots (viridis colour scale, blue = high richness vs yellow = low richness)

Open this repo in Cursor, then run chunks from top to bottom. If you render the
whole document, do so from the repo root so `here()` resolves paths correctly.

Reversed viridis variant: **`04b_RichnessLatitudeReversed.Rmd`**.

```{r}
# Core libraries for spatial + plotting
library(sf)
library(dplyr)
library(tidyverse)
library(here)
library(chondrichthyes.species.ranges)
library(future.apply)
library(hexbin)

# Plotting + layout helpers
library(patchwork)
library(rnaturalearth)
library(furrr)
library(purrr)
library(tictoc)
library(beepr)
# Load shared functions
source(here("mapping/00_functions.R"))

# 02_SpeciesRichnessMaps.Rmd maps + lat panels use theme_bw(); match grey20 panel box & axis ticks,
# with larger type than the default 11 pt base.
theme_richness_latitude <- function(base_size = 14, base_family = "") {
  theme_bw(base_size = base_size, base_family = base_family) +
    theme(
      panel.grid.major = element_blank(),
      panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
      panel.border = element_rect(fill = NA, colour = "grey20", linewidth = rel(1)),
      axis.ticks = element_line(colour = "grey20", linewidth = rel(0.5)),
      plot.title.position = "plot",
      plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", hjust = 0)
    )
}
```


```{r data_loading}
# Chunk name suggestion: data_loading
# Purpose: load precomputed spatial inputs used throughout this file.
# Load required spatial inputs. If these files are missing, run
# mapping/01_SpatialDataProcessing.Rmd first.
# This script expects:
# - Precomputed hex/ranges/metrics in data/processed
hex_path <- here("data/processed/hex.rds")
if (!file.exists(hex_path)) {
  stop("Missing data/processed/hex.rds. Run 01_SpatialDataProcessing.Rmd first.")
}
hex <- readRDS(hex_path)

# Species ranges (precomputed)
ranges_path <- here("data/processed/ranges.rds")
if (!file.exists(ranges_path)) {
  stop("Missing data/processed/ranges.rds. Run 01_SpatialDataProcessing.Rmd first.")
}
ranges <- readRDS(ranges_path)

# Skate/ray metric grids (precomputed)
hex_metrics_skates_path <- here("data/processed/hex_with_metrics_skates.rds")
hex_metrics_rays_path <- here("data/processed/hex_with_metrics_rays.rds")
if (!file.exists(hex_metrics_skates_path) || !file.exists(hex_metrics_rays_path)) {
  stop("Missing hex_with_metrics_*.rds. Run 01_SpatialDataProcessing.Rmd first.")
}
hex_with_metrics_skates <- readRDS(hex_metrics_skates_path)
hex_with_metrics_rays <- readRDS(hex_metrics_rays_path)

# Optional: unique-richness grid (precomputed)
hex_in_path <- here("data/processed/hex_in_richness_unique.rds")
hex_in <- if (file.exists(hex_in_path)) readRDS(hex_in_path) else NULL
```


```{r metrics_skates_rays}
# Chunk name suggestion: metrics_skates_rays
# Purpose: skate/ray metrics are loaded in data_loading from precomputed files.
```

```{r lat_richness_publication_plots}
# Chunk name suggestion: lat_richness_publication_plots
# Purpose: create publication-ready latitude–richness plots for skates and rays
# using precomputed hex metrics and dated PNG outputs.
date_tag <- format(Sys.Date(), "%y%m%d")

if (!exists("hex_with_metrics_skates") || !exists("hex_with_metrics_rays")) {
  hex_metrics_skates_path <- here::here("data/processed/hex_with_metrics_skates.rds")
  hex_metrics_rays_path <- here::here("data/processed/hex_with_metrics_rays.rds")
  if (!file.exists(hex_metrics_skates_path) || !file.exists(hex_metrics_rays_path)) {
    stop("Missing hex_with_metrics_*.rds. Run 01_SpatialDataProcessing.Rmd first.")
  }
  hex_with_metrics_skates <- readRDS(hex_metrics_skates_path)
  hex_with_metrics_rays <- readRDS(hex_metrics_rays_path)
}

add_lat_lon <- function(sf_obj) {
  # Compute centroid points and attach lon/lat (WGS84) to the sf object
  pts <- st_point_on_surface(sf_obj)
  pts_ll <- st_transform(pts, 4326)
  coords <- st_coordinates(pts_ll)
  sf_obj$lon <- coords[, "X"]
  sf_obj$lat <- coords[, "Y"]
  sf_obj
}

plot_lat_richness <- function(sf_obj, title_text) {
  # Drop geometry, remove zeros, and bin latitude into 5-degree bands
  # Upper-envelope line follows 02_SpeciesRichnessMaps.Rmd (loess on bin-wise 95th %ile)
  plot_df <- sf_obj %>%
    st_drop_geometry() %>%
    dplyr::ungroup() %>%
    mutate(lat = as.numeric(lat)) %>%
    filter(is.finite(lat), is.finite(species_richness), species_richness > 0) %>%
    mutate(lat_bin = floor(lat / 5) * 5)

  p95_df <- plot_df %>%
    group_by(lat_bin) %>%
    summarise(
      lat_mid = dplyr::first(lat_bin) + 2.5,
      richness_p95 = quantile(species_richness, 0.95, na.rm = TRUE),
      .groups = "drop"
    ) %>%
    arrange(lat_mid)

  loess_fit <- loess(richness_p95 ~ lat_mid, data = p95_df, span = 0.5)
  p95_df$richness_fit <- pmax(0, predict(loess_fit, newdata = p95_df))

  # Check axis ranges (x = richness, y = latitude)
  x_range <- range(plot_df$species_richness, na.rm = TRUE)
  y_range <- range(plot_df$lat, na.rm = TRUE)
  message(sprintf("Richness range: %.2f to %.2f", x_range[1], x_range[2]))
  message(sprintf("Latitude range: %.2f to %.2f", y_range[1], y_range[2]))

  ggplot(plot_df, aes(x = species_richness, y = lat)) +
    # Solid, slightly larger points colored by richness (yellow -> blue)
    geom_point(aes(color = species_richness), size = 1.2, alpha = 1) +
    scale_color_viridis_c(option = "D", direction = -1) +
    geom_path(
      data = p95_df,
      aes(x = richness_fit, y = lat_mid),
      color = "grey40",
      linewidth = 2.1,
      inherit.aes = FALSE
    ) +
    coord_cartesian(xlim = c(0, 80), ylim = c(-90, 90), expand = FALSE) +
    scale_y_continuous(
      breaks = c(-90, -60, -30, 0, 30, 60, 90),
      labels = c("90°S", "60°S", "30°S", "0°", "30°N", "60°N", "90°N")
    ) +
    theme_richness_latitude() +
    theme(
      legend.position = "inside",
      legend.position.inside = c(0.98, 0.5),
      legend.justification = c(1, 0.5),
      legend.background = element_rect(fill = "white", color = NA),
      legend.margin = margin(6, 6, 6, 6)
    ) +
    labs(
      title = title_text,
      x = "Species richness",
      y = "Latitude",
      color = "Richness"
    )
}

hex_with_metrics_skates <- add_lat_lon(hex_with_metrics_skates)
hex_with_metrics_rays <- add_lat_lon(hex_with_metrics_rays)

skate_plot <- plot_lat_richness(
  hex_with_metrics_skates,
  "A."
)

ray_plot <- plot_lat_richness(
  hex_with_metrics_rays,
  "B."
)

# Combine with skate above ray and shared x-range
lat_richness_combined <- skate_plot / ray_plot

# Combined figure (skates above rays)
ggsave(
  filename = here::here(sprintf("mapping/figures/lat_richness_combined_%s.png", date_tag)),
  plot = lat_richness_combined,
  width = 7,
  height = 10,
  units = "in",
  dpi = 600
)

# Save PNGs with date tag (yymmdd) for reproducibility
ggsave(
  filename = here::here(sprintf("mapping/figures/skate_richness_latitude_%s.png", date_tag)),
  plot = skate_plot,
  width = 7,
  height = 5,
  units = "in",
  dpi = 600
)

ggsave(
  filename = here::here(sprintf("mapping/figures/ray_richness_latitude_%s.png", date_tag)),
  plot = ray_plot,
  width = 7,
  height = 5,
  units = "in",
  dpi = 600
)
```

```{r lat_on_x_richness_on_y}
# Chunk name suggestion: lat_on_x_richness_on_y
# Purpose: plot latitude on x and richness on y with loess; export combined layouts.

plot_lat_richness_xy <- function(sf_obj, title_text, y_label) {
  plot_df <- sf_obj %>%
    st_drop_geometry() %>%
    filter(is.finite(lat), is.finite(species_richness), species_richness > 0) %>%
    mutate(lat_bin = floor(lat / 5) * 5)

  # Upper-centile envelope to smooth (edit to explore sensitivity)
  centile <- 0.90
  centile_df <- plot_df %>%
    group_by(lat_bin) %>%
    summarise(
      lat_mid = lat_bin + 2.5,
      richness_centile = quantile(species_richness, centile, na.rm = TRUE),
      .groups = "drop"
    ) %>%
    mutate(richness_centile = pmax(0, richness_centile))

  ggplot(plot_df, aes(x = lat, y = species_richness)) +
    geom_point(aes(color = species_richness), size = 1.2, alpha = 1) +
    scale_color_viridis_c(option = "D", direction = -1) +
    geom_smooth(
      data = centile_df,
      aes(x = lat_mid, y = richness_centile),
      method = "loess",
      span = 0.25,
      se = FALSE,
      color = "grey25",
      linewidth = 1.5,
      inherit.aes = FALSE
    ) +
    coord_cartesian(xlim = c(-90, 90), ylim = c(0, 80), expand = FALSE) +
    scale_x_continuous(
      breaks = c(-90, -60, -30, 0, 30, 60, 90),
      labels = c("90°S", "60°S", "30°S", "0°", "30°N", "60°N", "90°N")
    ) +
    theme_richness_latitude() +
    theme(
      legend.position = "inside",
      legend.position.inside = c(0.98, 0.5),
      legend.justification = c(1, 0.5),
      legend.background = element_rect(fill = "white", color = NA),
      legend.margin = margin(6, 6, 6, 6)
    ) +
    labs(
      title = title_text,
      x = "Latitude",
      y = y_label,
      color = "Richness"
    )
}

skate_plot_xy <- plot_lat_richness_xy(
  hex_with_metrics_skates,
  "A.",
  "Skate richness"
)
ray_plot_xy <- plot_lat_richness_xy(
  hex_with_metrics_rays,
  "B.",
  "Ray richness"
)

# Stack: skates on top, rays on bottom
lat_x_stack <- skate_plot_xy / ray_plot_xy

# Side-by-side: skates left, rays right
lat_x_side <- skate_plot_xy | ray_plot_xy

ggsave(
  filename = here::here(sprintf("mapping/figures/lat_x_richness_y_stack_%s.png", date_tag)),
  plot = lat_x_stack,
  width = 7,
  height = 10,
  units = "in",
  dpi = 600
)

ggsave(
  filename = here::here(sprintf("mapping/figures/lat_x_richness_y_side_%s.png", date_tag)),
  plot = lat_x_side,
  width = 10,
  height = 5,
  units = "in",
  dpi = 600
)
```

```{r lat_on_x_richness_on_y_no_labels}
# Chunk name suggestion: lat_on_x_richness_on_y_no_labels
# Purpose: plot latitude on x and richness on y with loess; export layouts
# without titles or axis labels (colour legend on the right).

plot_lat_richness_xy_nolabels <- function(sf_obj) {
  plot_df <- sf_obj %>%
    st_drop_geometry() %>%
    filter(is.finite(lat), is.finite(species_richness), species_richness > 0) %>%
    mutate(lat_bin = floor(lat / 5) * 5)

  # Upper-centile envelope to smooth (edit to explore sensitivity)
  centile <- 0.90
  centile_df <- plot_df %>%
    group_by(lat_bin) %>%
    summarise(
      lat_mid = lat_bin + 2.5,
      richness_centile = quantile(species_richness, centile, na.rm = TRUE),
      .groups = "drop"
    ) %>%
    mutate(richness_centile = pmax(0, richness_centile))

  ggplot(plot_df, aes(x = lat, y = species_richness)) +
    geom_point(aes(color = species_richness), size = 1.2, alpha = 1) +
    scale_color_viridis_c(option = "D", direction = -1) +
    geom_smooth(
      data = centile_df,
      aes(x = lat_mid, y = richness_centile),
      method = "loess",
      span = 0.4,
      se = FALSE,
      color = "grey25",
      linewidth = 1.5,
      inherit.aes = FALSE
    ) +
    coord_cartesian(xlim = c(-90, 90), ylim = c(0, 80), expand = FALSE) +
    scale_x_continuous(
      breaks = c(-90, -60, -30, 0, 30, 60, 90),
      labels = c("90°S", "60°S", "30°S", "0°", "30°N", "60°N", "90°N")
    ) +
    theme_richness_latitude() +
    theme(
      legend.position = "inside",
      legend.position.inside = c(0.98, 0.5),
      legend.justification = c(1, 0.5),
      legend.background = element_rect(fill = "white", color = NA),
      legend.margin = margin(6, 6, 6, 6),
      plot.title = element_blank(),
      axis.title = element_blank()
    ) +
    labs(color = "Richness")
}

skate_plot_xy_nolabels <- plot_lat_richness_xy_nolabels(hex_with_metrics_skates)
ray_plot_xy_nolabels <- plot_lat_richness_xy_nolabels(hex_with_metrics_rays)

# Stack: skates on top, rays on bottom
lat_x_stack_nolabels <- skate_plot_xy_nolabels / ray_plot_xy_nolabels

# Side-by-side: skates left, rays right
lat_x_side_nolabels <- skate_plot_xy_nolabels | ray_plot_xy_nolabels

ggsave(
  filename = here::here(sprintf("mapping/figures/lat_x_richness_y_stack_%s.png", date_tag)),
  plot = lat_x_stack_nolabels,
  width = 7,
  height = 10,
  units = "in",
  dpi = 600
)

ggsave(
  filename = here::here(sprintf("mapping/figures/lat_x_richness_y_side_%s.png", date_tag)),
  plot = lat_x_side_nolabels,
  width = 10,
  height = 5,
  units = "in",
  dpi = 600
)
```

```{r lat_on_x_richness_on_y_loess_peak}
# Chunk name suggestion: lat_on_x_richness_on_y_loess_peak
# Purpose: identify latitude(s) where the loess envelope reaches its peak.
# Handles unimodal and bimodal curves by finding local maxima and splitting
# peaks by hemisphere.

build_centile_envelope <- function(sf_obj, centile = 0.90) {
  plot_df <- sf_obj %>%
    st_drop_geometry() %>%
    filter(is.finite(lat), is.finite(species_richness), species_richness > 0) %>%
    mutate(lat_bin = floor(lat / 5) * 5)

  plot_df %>%
    group_by(lat_bin) %>%
    reframe(
      lat_mid = lat_bin + 2.5,
      richness_centile = quantile(species_richness, centile, na.rm = TRUE)
    ) %>%
    mutate(richness_centile = pmax(0, richness_centile))
}

predict_loess_curve <- function(centile_df, span = 0.4, lat_step = 0.1) {
  loess_fit <- loess(richness_centile ~ lat_mid, data = centile_df, span = span)
  lat_seq <- seq(-90, 90, by = lat_step)
  preds <- predict(loess_fit, newdata = data.frame(lat_mid = lat_seq))
  tibble(lat = lat_seq, pred = preds)
}

find_local_maxima <- function(curve_df, tol = 0.01) {
  df <- curve_df %>%
    filter(is.finite(pred))

  if (nrow(df) < 3) {
    return(tibble(peak_lat = numeric(0), peak_value = numeric(0)))
  }

  d1 <- diff(df$pred)
  sign_change <- d1[-1] <= 0 & d1[-length(d1)] > 0
  idx <- which(sign_change) + 1

  peaks <- df[idx, , drop = FALSE]
  if (nrow(peaks) == 0) {
    max_val <- max(df$pred, na.rm = TRUE)
    peak_lats <- df$lat[abs(df$pred - max_val) <= tol]
    return(tibble(
      peak_lat = if (length(peak_lats) > 0) peak_lats else df$lat[which.max(df$pred)],
      peak_value = max_val
    ))
  }

  # Expand peaks to plateaus within tolerance of each local max
  peaks <- peaks %>%
    mutate(
      peak_value = pred,
      peak_lat = lat
    ) %>%
    select(peak_lat, peak_value)

  peaks
}

summarise_peaks <- function(peaks_df, group_label) {
  if (nrow(peaks_df) == 0) {
    return(tibble(
      group = group_label,
      peak_type = "none",
      peak_lat_min = NA_real_,
      peak_lat_max = NA_real_,
      peak_value = NA_real_
    ))
  }

  peaks_df %>%
    mutate(
      peak_type = case_when(
        peak_lat < 0 ~ "south",
        peak_lat > 0 ~ "north",
        TRUE ~ "equator"
      )
    ) %>%
    group_by(peak_type) %>%
    summarise(
      group = group_label,
      peak_lat_min = min(peak_lat, na.rm = TRUE),
      peak_lat_max = max(peak_lat, na.rm = TRUE),
      peak_value = max(peak_value, na.rm = TRUE),
      .groups = "drop"
    )
}

# tol controls how tightly peak plateaus are defined (smaller = narrower range)
find_loess_peaks <- function(sf_obj, group_label, centile = 0.90, span = 0.4, lat_step = 0.1, tol = 0.005) {
  centile_df <- build_centile_envelope(sf_obj, centile = centile)
  curve_df <- predict_loess_curve(centile_df, span = span, lat_step = lat_step)
  peaks_df <- find_local_maxima(curve_df, tol = tol)
  summarise_peaks(peaks_df, group_label)
}

peak_summary <- bind_rows(
  find_loess_peaks(hex_with_metrics_skates, "Skates"),
  find_loess_peaks(hex_with_metrics_rays, "Rays")
)

# Summary columns:
# - peak_type: hemisphere of each local maximum (south/north/equator)
# - peak_lat_min/peak_lat_max: latitude range for a peak (plateau if range > 0)
# - peak_value: max loess envelope value within that peak range
print(peak_summary)
```

```{r lat_richness_hexbin}
# Chunk name suggestion: lat_richness_hexbin
# Purpose: hexbin plots of richness vs latitude for skates and rays.

if (!requireNamespace("hexbin", quietly = TRUE)) {
  stop("Package 'hexbin' is required for geom_hex(). Install with install.packages('hexbin').")
}

plot_lat_richness_hexbin <- function(sf_obj, title_text, y_label) {
  plot_df <- sf_obj %>%
    st_drop_geometry() %>%
    filter(is.finite(lat), is.finite(species_richness), species_richness > 0)

  ggplot(plot_df, aes(x = lat, y = species_richness)) +
    # Hexbin density (bins controls resolution)
    geom_hex(bins = 40) +
    scale_fill_viridis_c(option = "D", direction = -1) +
    coord_cartesian(xlim = c(-90, 90), ylim = c(0, 80), expand = FALSE) +
    scale_x_continuous(
      breaks = c(-90, -60, -30, 0, 30, 60, 90),
      labels = c("90°S", "60°S", "30°S", "0°", "30°N", "60°N", "90°N")
    ) +
    theme_richness_latitude() +
    theme(
      legend.position = "inside",
      legend.position.inside = c(0.98, 0.5),
      legend.justification = c(1, 0.5),
      legend.background = element_rect(fill = "white", color = NA),
      legend.margin = margin(6, 6, 6, 6)
    ) +
    labs(
      title = title_text,
      x = "Latitude",
      y = y_label,
      fill = "Count"
    )
}

skate_hexbin <- plot_lat_richness_hexbin(hex_with_metrics_skates, "A.", "Skate richness")
ray_hexbin <- plot_lat_richness_hexbin(hex_with_metrics_rays, "B.", "Ray richness")

lat_hexbin_stack <- skate_hexbin / ray_hexbin
lat_hexbin_side <- skate_hexbin | ray_hexbin

# Stacked and side-by-side outputs
ggsave(
  filename = here::here(sprintf("mapping/figures/lat_richness_hexbin_stack_%s.png", date_tag)),
  plot = lat_hexbin_stack,
  width = 7,
  height = 10,
  units = "in",
  dpi = 600
)

ggsave(
  filename = here::here(sprintf("mapping/figures/lat_richness_hexbin_side_%s.png", date_tag)),
  plot = lat_hexbin_side,
  width = 10,
  height = 5,
  units = "in",
  dpi = 600
)
```
